reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures Munkres Exercise 30.10. The index type ℕ is the standard Lean encoding of a "countable product" (countably infinite index); the finite case is trivial and is the conventional implicit reading in this context. Each `X i` carries a `TopologicalSpace` instance, and the hypothesis `∀ i, ∃ s, Countable s ∧ Dense s` exactly says each factor has a countable dense subset (Mathlib's `Countable` on sets = at-most-countable via subtype; `Dense` = closure is everything). The conclusion asserts the same property of `Π i, X i`, matching the informal claim. Quantifier structure, hypotheses, and ambient types all align.
